summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Significance test for μ'?

"Significance test for μ" means: A formal procedure using t = (x̄ - μ₀)/(s/√n) to decide whether sample evidence contradicts H₀: μ = μ₀. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Type I error'?

"Type I error" means: Rejecting H₀ when it is actually true; probability equals α, the significance level. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Type II error'?

"Type II error" means: Failing to reject H₀ when Hₐ is actually true; probability denoted β. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Power'?

"Power" means: The probability of correctly rejecting H₀ when Hₐ is true; Power = 1 - β; increased by larger n, larger effect size, or larger α. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Effect size'?

"Effect size" means: The magnitude of the difference between the true parameter and the null value; determines the practical importance of a statistically significant result. The other options describe different concepts.
